title: Future of Influence
gist: BCG finds the consumer purchase path has fragmented into research-led journeys spanning 15+ touchpoints, with social media and GenAI emerging as trusted advisers, forcing brands to win at generative/answer engine optimization (GEO/AEO) and make websites machine-readable as a new form of digital shelf space.

insights.claim: A BCG Global Consumer Radar survey found approximately 50% of consumers approach a purchase without a predetermined brand preference, including 43% who want to research and compare options.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Half of purchases are up for grabs at the research stage, raising the strategic value of discovery-stage influence over brand-loyalty spend.
insights.quote: approximately 50% of consumers approach their purchase without a predetermined brand preference

insights.claim: The number of touchpoints consumers encounter has jumped from historically about 5 to more than 15 today, with digital channels outperforming traditional media in information discovery and purchase conversion by 9 and 13 percentage points respectively.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Touchpoint proliferation shifts marketing ROI decisively toward digital discovery channels; legacy media budgets are structurally pressured.
insights.quote: Historically, consumers interacted with about 5 touchpoints; today, they may encounter more than 15.
insights.locator: In addition, the proliferation of digital channels

insights.claim: Research-led journeys are not limited to the young: more than 50% of Gen-Z and millennial journeys are research-led, but so are 42% of journeys by consumers aged 45 or older, and the norm for over 40% of consumers worldwide.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: The research-led shift is broad-based across age and geography, so brands cannot treat it as a niche youth phenomenon.
insights.quote: so are 42% of the journeys undertaken by consumers aged 45 or older

themes.theme: Social media and GenAI as trusted advisers

insights.claim: With 97% of internet users active on social platforms at least monthly, social media is used to gather information in 22% of research-led journeys, and depending on category 90% of consumers who engage with it rank it among their five most influential touchpoints.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Social is now a 'must-win' research and conversion channel, not just communication; presence is table stakes for brand consideration.
insights.quote: social media plays a direct role in the discovery and evaluation phases. In 22% of research-led consumer journeys, this channel is used to gather information
insights.locator: Social media has become a “must win” channel

insights.claim: The share of consumers using LLM research has more than doubled in recent years, with 13% of research-led consumers now interacting with GenAI tools before purchase, a level approaching traditional TV advertising.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: GenAI is rapidly reaching parity with TV as a pre-purchase influence channel; LLM visibility is becoming a measurable marketing battleground.
insights.quote: Already, 13% of research-led consumers interact with GenAI tools before making a purchase, a level approaching that of traditional TV advertising.
insights.locator: AI and LLM research are rapidly emerging

insights.claim: 85% of consumers who interact with GenAI tools rank them among their top five most influential touchpoints, and 60% say they trust AI-generated results, citing clarity, objectivity, and personalized responses.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: High trust in AI results means LLMs are becoming gatekeepers of consideration; brands lose control of the narrative to model outputs.
insights.quote: 60% of consumers say they trust AI-generated results, citing clarity, objectivity, and personalized responses as key advantages
insights.locator: AI and LLM research are rapidly emerging

themes.theme: Implications for brand value and strategy

insights.claim: BCG argues visibility in LLM-generated responses is becoming a new and necessary form of digital shelf space, but brands will be judged not only on how they show up but on what they can credibly claim and consistently deliver.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: AI 'shelf space' is the next SEO-style competitive arena; brands that optimize for LLM citation and back claims with delivery will capture share.
insights.quote: visibility in LLM-generated responses is becoming a new and necessary form of digital shelf space
insights.locator: For brands, the emergence of AI-driven discovery

insights.claim: BCG contends that even as algorithmic comparison sharpens, a strong brand still encodes emotion, identity, aspiration, and instinct that a list of features cannot capture, preserving brand value when AI agents can transact on a consumer's behalf.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Pushes back on the thesis that AI commoditizes brands; emotional brand equity remains a moat even in agent-mediated purchasing.
insights.quote: a strong brand still encodes emotion, identity, aspiration, and instinct, all of which still shape what a consumer reaches for
insights.locator: This shift also forces a harder question

insights.claim: BCG recommends brands shift to a fast content value chain delivering new content in no more than a week rather than the six months traditionally required, and make websites machine-readable to prepare for the agent-to-agent journey.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Speed of content production and machine-readable sites become operational differentiators in an AI-mediated discovery world.
insights.quote: delivering new content in no more than a week, rather than the six months or more that has traditionally been required
insights.locator: How do I build a fast-acting

insights.claim: BCG notes one multibrand retailer that adopted GEO and AEO realized a measurable payoff, and warns that because LLM models are very rational and critical, brands must articulate functional, technical, and emotional benefits clearly.
insights.speaker: Gaby Barrios
insights.so_what: Early GEO/AEO adopters are already seeing returns; content must satisfy rational LLM evaluation, favoring factual, structured product information.
insights.quote: LLM models are very rational and critical
insights.locator: How do I build a fast-acting
